steps.title: Read the original center
steps.content:
A circle in the form $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$ has center $(h,k)$. For circle A,
$$(x-7)^2+(y+3)^2=1$$
so the center is $(7,-3)$.

steps.title: Translate the center right
steps.content:
Moving right $4$ units adds $4$ to the $x$-coordinate and leaves the $y$-coordinate unchanged.
$$(7+4,-3)=(11,-3)$$

steps.title: Write the translated equation
steps.content:
The radius stays the same, so the new circle has center $(11,-3)$ and radius $1$. Its equation is
$$(x-11)^2+(y+3)^2=1$$
The answer is C.
